---
title: "DDClient updating internal IP to dyndns"
date: 2007-08-11
forum: Server Platforms
---

### Post by HautingLu on 2007-08-11
Hi,

I've been using ipcheck with a cron recently to update my IP to dyndns. Though I've noticed that my L/P show up in the open if I time "ps -ef" correctly.

So I installed ddclient and put in my settings. It seems to work except **it updates my local up address**: 192.xxx.xxx.03.

This is my ddclient.conf file:

> 
# Configuration file for ddclient generated by debconf
#
# /etc/ddclient.conf

pid=/var/run/ddclient.pid
protocol=dyndns2
use=if, if=eth0
server=members.dyndns.org
login=******
password=******
xyz.dyndns.org


Is there to configure it find my external (router) IP?

Thanks.

---

### Post by HautingLu on 2007-08-11
Problem solved:

Commented out:
> 
## via hardware interface (if you don't have a router/firewall)
#use=if, if=eth0

and added it: 
> 
## via our CheckIP server
use=web, web=checkip.dyndns.com/, web-skip='IP Address'

---

### Post by marx2k on 2008-01-19
Thank you! This helped tremendously!
